title: Stop Funding Global Terrorists Act of 2025
short_title: Stop Funding Global Terrorists Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill stops the United States from giving money to the United Nations to help Afghanistan. The US cannot give this money until the Secretary of State makes a promise to Congress about two things. First, no American money will be sent as cash into Afghanistan by the UN. Second, no terrorist groups will get any of this money from the cash shipments. If the Secretary later finds out their promise was wrong, they must take back their promise and tell Congress why.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Stop Funding Global Terrorists Act of twenty twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Restriction on Funding for United Nations
sections.text: The United States cannot give any money to the United Nations for help in Afghanistan until the Secretary of State makes a promise in writing to certain committees in Congress. The Secretary must promise three things. First, no American money is used when the United Nations sends cash into Afghanistan. Second, no group that has been officially labeled as a specially designated global terrorist organization gets money from these cash shipments. Third, no group that has been officially labeled as a foreign terrorist organization gets money from these cash shipments. If the Secretary later finds out that their written promise was wrong, they must take back that promise. They must then tell the congressional committees that they are taking it back and explain in detail why they are doing so. In this section, appropriate congressional committees means the Committee on Foreign Relations and the Committee on Appropriations in the Senate, plus the Committee on Foreign Affairs and the Committee on Appropriations in the House of Representatives. A foreign terrorist organization means a group that the Secretary of State has officially labeled as a foreign terrorist organization under section two hundred nineteen of the Immigration and Nationality Act. A specially designated global terrorist organization means a group that has been officially labeled as a specially designated global terrorist under Executive Order thirteen two two four, which is about blocking property and stopping business deals with people who commit terrorism, threaten to commit terrorism, or support terrorism.
